messages.content: You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient is sleeping in bed. He lets out a loud groan and has versive head turn to the right. He has right upper extremity extension with left upper extremity flexion, followed by tonic-clonic activity. Later, the patient is unable to remember events preceding this seizure. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient is sleeping bed. He starts screaming and turns slightly to the left. Then he returns to sleep. Later, the patient is unaware of this seizure. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient is sleeping in bed. He opens his eyes and has versive head turn to the right. He has right upper extremity extension with left upper extremity flexion, followed by tonic-clonic activity. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Her arms are covered by the blanket but it seems like the right arm is moving. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.

Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.

Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Patient is in the bed and asleep when she suddenly turns her head to the left - non versive; has left leg greater than arm automatisms. She opens here eyes and looks around. She places her left arm over the right hand and shuffles in the bed or moves her trunk with some bilateral leg movements. She was not tested for awareness during the episode. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

She is sitting in bed and suddenly postures with her right hand flexed in a dystonic posture near her head while her left hand is bed. Then she flexes her right hand towards her body while her left hand extends and her eyes deviate to the left and she begins having clonic activity mainly with her left side. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient becomes unresponsive to his mother and points with his left index finger with left arm external rotation. This is then followed by high frequency movements of his head, flexion at the hips, and right head version. He then develops rhythmic jerking movements of all extremities with subsequent figure four position with right upper extremity extended or left upper extrimity flexed and right lower extrimity extended or left lower extrimity flexed. This is followed by tonic extension of all extremities with subsequent clonic jerking movements. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient is sitting in bed when he begins to exhibit bilateral arm external rotation movements and high frequency rhythmic head jerking movements. He the exhibits tonic head deviation to the right with subsequent figure 4 position with right upper extremity extended and left upper extrimity flexed, also right lower extrimity extended and left lower extrimity flexed. This is followed by tonic extension of all limbs with subsequent clonic jerking. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient exhibits very rapid right upper extremity finger movements with decreased verbal responsiveness. Rhythmic activity ceases briefly and is then followed first by head jerking then quickly also with left upper extrimity then bilateral symmetric arm external rotation jerks. This is followed by head turn to the right with very high frequency movements; he then exhibits a figure four position of upper extremities with right upper extremity extension and left upper extrimity flexion prior to bilateral UE tonic extension and clonic movements. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

Eyes widen, looking around, fidgeting, then forced left head deviation, ictal cry, then flexion of bilateral upper extremities Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

One of her visitors reported that the patient appeared to have a 'blank stare' and suddenly jumps back to awareness. Her care giver reports possibly a typical event. During rounds, patient reported possibly a typical event. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
NES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient is awake throughout the video. He looks down and begins exhibiting limb automatisms as he taps his right fingers on his right knee in a repetitive, purposeless manner. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
NES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient is awake and verbally responsive throughout the video. He looks down and begins exhibiting limb automatisms as his right fingers and right arm shake at varying frequencies throughout the event. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
NES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

Semiologically, this was marked by the patient staring for a few seconds blankly into space. He then reanimated and pushed the call button to report the staring episode. The patient could not recall having this episode or pushing the call button when asked about it on rounds the following day. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
NES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient pushed the button to report a weird feeling of his head feeling compressed. He also reported headache. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
NES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient had another episode of whole-body shaking at 23:30. The patient was noted to push the call button beforehand. He then developed irregular body trembling. Eyes were closed. This was accompanied by moaning throughout the episode. Afterwards, he was noted to reanimate and was able to speak. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
NES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient had another episode of whole-body shaking. The patient was noted to push the call button beforehand. He then developed irregular body trembling. Eyes were closed. This was accompanied by moaning throughout the episode. He was able to follow instructions during evaluation while nursing evaluation. Afterwards, he was noted to reanimate and was able to speak. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
NES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient is verbally responsive as he interacts with staff over the intercome. He also exhibits some purposeless tongue and lip movements, as well as simultaneous, asynchronous shaking of the arms. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
NES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

Patient awake, supine on bed before clinical onset. During event patient would remain with eyes open and would have intermittent bilateral asynchronous upper limbs tremors and intermittent yawning, limp smacking. Patient remained otherwise motionless for 2-3 minutes when suddenly he shook his head and pressed button to verbalize "I just had a seizure" Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
NES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

Patient awake, supine on bed, and eating before clinical onset. After chewing, he is noted to start staring with occasional chewing movements continuing. He keeps staring ahead and breathing, and at one point wipes his mouth with a napkin. He subsequently continues staring and reaches for the call light, though does not activate it. After a bit he abruptly and briefly shakes his head after which he hits to call button to indicate that “I just had a staring seizure.” The patient was not tested during the event. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
NES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

Patient lying supine in bed, awake. Noted to have some chewing movements. Then rhythmic tremulousness is noted in arms and torso. Patient says “Not now!” The frequency and amplitude of the shaking increases, during which time the patient reaches for the call button. The shaking increases in amplitude and then becomes asymmetric with the patient arrhythmically cycling his right forearm to keep hitting up against his abdomen and chest repeatedly. He is responsive to the monitoring tech remotely while the shaking is ongoing and says “I’m having seizure.” Near the end of the clinical event, he start to cry “no, no, no” and continues to have the cycling movement of his right hand, which ultimately abruptly ends. Following the event the patient is noted to have slow breathing and appears tired. He is initially unresponsive to verbal questioning but becomes responsive again after a short time. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
NES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

Patient awake, seated on chair before clinical onset. He was alone at room when the physician entered. He remained motionless and when initially addressed, he did not answered. Afterwards, he shook head and was able to follow instructions and respond accurately to questions while he had intermittent left greater than right upper limbs shaking movements. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
NES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

From sleep, the patient opens his eyes then blinks repetitively follow by head elevation and non-versive right head turn. He then exhibits rapid bilateral tonic extension and clonic activity. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

Patient is sleep and suddenly wakes with mild right head turn followed by bilateral tonic extension and clonic acitivity. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

There is behavioral arrest followed by right hand automatisms then bilateral hand movements and oral automatisms. This is followed by covering his mouth with his left hand then tonic extension of his right arm, rightward head deviation, facial grimace, and figure four motion with the left arm flexed and right arm extended with subsequent tonic extension of extremities followed by clonic movements. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

From sleep, the patient exhibits a brief oral movements (perhaps a smile), not well seen due to obscuration from blankets, followed by a grimace, yell, and turn onto his back. This is followed by a figure four position with his right arm flexed and left arm extended with subsequent bilateral tonic extension and clonic jerking movements. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient exhibits a grin with repetitive blinking and some rocking back and forth in bed. He then exhibits a versive head turn to the left with subsequent tonic posturing and figure four with right arm flexed and left arm extended followed by tonic extensions and clonic jerking. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient has just completed photic simulation and was hyperventilating. She stares and is unresponsive to written prompts. After about 30 seconds, she blinks a few times and rubs her eyes and is able to follow written command to point to the ceiling. Then she becomes unresponsive again and left upper extremity is limp to passive movement. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
NES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient exhibits some behavioral arrest with repetitive grunting sounds. This is followed by right clonic facial pulling, right eye deviation, and right head turn. This is followed by involvement of the right upper extremity and right lower extremity and subsequently the patient exhibits right upper extremity extension and left upper extremity flexion at the elbow, with an appearance of "Figure 4". Left sided extremities are subsequently involved prior to offset. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

She is talking to registered nurse before onset, lowers herself in bed, and then is sitting and starring at 11:20:32. She smiles and laughs at 11:20:43, followed by eyes version to right, then clonic arms followed by versive head and body to left by 11:20:54. She then extends her right arm and generalizes. Seizure ends at 11:22:12. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient is laying in bed and his mom is giving him a massage at 14:15:32, he exhibits oroalimentary automatisms at 14:15:34, he exhibits dyscognitive language symptoms at 14:15:50. Right manual gestural automatisms are evident at 14:16:06, and bimanual gestural automatisms occur at 14:16:11, he looks to the left at 14:16:14, and the seizure terminates at 14:18:03. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient exhibits dyscognitive language symptoms at 09:21:51 Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient presses the button to report an aura at 08:47:35, he nods yes at 08:55:39, he grabs a cup of coffee at 08:55:43, and he exhibits bimanual gestural automatisms at 08:55:59. He exhibits dyscognitive language and executive function at 08:56:18, and bimanual gestural automatisms at 08:56:26, seizure termination occurs at 08:58:15. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

She is sleeping prior to seizure. Arousal seen at 22:45:07, she then looks around, followed by right hand automatisms. She then sighs, non responsive to sister. It ends at 22:46:20. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

patient sitting in bed, looking down, then versive head turn to left, general tonic clonic with moaning and right face twitching. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

patient sitting in bed watching TV, looks down briefly then versive head turn to left followed by general tonic clonic with moaning and right face twitching. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

patient laying in bed, was in process of having intravenus changed out. Versive head turn to left followed by generalized tonic clonic with moaning and right face twitching. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient performed hyperventilation to trigger this event. Her right lower face has a sustained contraction, pulling her lip towards the right. There is intermittent subtle twitching in her cheek. She is able to follow simple commands, nod appropriately, repeats "Mary had a little lamb", and state that her episodes usually last 2-3 minutes. The contraction gradually subsides and the event lasts about 5 minutes. The patient endorses a tired feeling in her legs and states that this was a more intense version of her typical event at home. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
NES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

Patient experiences indescribable aura followed by non-rhythmic motions of her bilateral lower extremities, she then has head version to the right with non-rhythmic jerking of her bilateral upper extremities. The patient retained awareness throughout event. This event last seven minutes. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

the patient was awake laying on his left side when the electrographic seizure begins and he vocalizes. He continues to vocalize and he turns to the midline and postures his limbs and progresses into a generalized tonic clonic seizure. His eyes look like they be looking to the right. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient is asleep, and at 5:11 eyes open and the patient slowly sits up toward the right side of the bed. At 5:12 there is left head version and left gaze. About 15 seconds later evolves into generalized tonic clonic seizure, lasting about 50 seconds. The patient then appears confused and closes eyes, eventually going back to sleep, however the EEG remains active. At 5:51, she opens eyes again and sits up, and about 1 min later has left head version followed by generalized tonic clonic seizure, followed by post-ictal state. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient is asleep, and at 5:11 eyes open and the patient slowly sits up toward the right side of the bed. At 5:12 there is left head version and left gaze. About 15 seconds later evolves into generalized tonic clonic seizure, lasting about 50 seconds. The patient then appears confused and closes eyes, eventually going back to sleep, however the EEG remains active. At 5:51, she opens eyes again and sits up, and about 1 min later has left head version followed by generalized tonic clonic seizure, followed by post-ictal state. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES

He is lying in bed, opens eyes at 10:59:42, and puts the water glass down with right hand. He soon has right arm tonic posturing followed by grunting noise followed by facial automatisms. The seizure ends on EEG but he continues to be confused and does not follow commands for 15 -20 minutes later. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

He is lying in bed looking around when seizure begins. At 13:49:57 facial automatisms noted followed by right head version, right arm tonic posturing and left arm gestural automatisms. Followed by tonic clonic movements of upper extremities. He does not completely generalize. Post seizure he is confused and fumbling around. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient has an arousal at 03:34:01, he looks up and to the left at 03:34:03, he has a head turn to the right at 03:34:05, he exhibits oroalimentary automatisms at 03:34:35, he vocalizes at 03:34:48, he has a head turn right at 03:34:51, he has a right face pull at 03:35:02, he exhibits right sided hemi-tonic-clonic movements at 03:35:13, the seizure generalized seconds later, and then terminates at 03:35:46. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). Answer with 'ES' or 'NES’ and do not include any other text.
ES
You are a medical assistant helping to observe, describe, and analyze seizure videos.

The patient has an arousal at 06:18:41,this is followed by bimanual gestural automatisms at 06:18:50, and oroalimentary automatisms at 06:19:15, a head turn right is evident at 06:19:18, this is followed by a vocalization at 06:19:18, and generalization at 06:19:34. Based on the patient’s seizure video and seizure semiology report, determine whether the patient has epileptic seizures (ES) or non-epileptic events (NES). {"answer": "NES", "description": "The patient had multiple of her type I episodes. These consisted of the patient loudly calling out “mama,” followed by abduction of the shoulders bilaterally and flapping arm movements bilaterally. The patient would sometimes cross the arms slightly during the flapping movements. Sometimes the arm flapping would be more prominent on one side or the other (never consistently one). The flapping of the arms would wax and wane for seconds to minutes prior to termination. Sometimes there would be more prominent extension of the left upper extremity and flexion at the right elbow during the events. During the arm flapping, the patient would have eyes opening and make intermittent crying or moaning noises. There would also sometimes be head shaking from side to side. Sometimes the legs would also abduct or adduct at the thighs during the events. Sometimes there would also be pelvic thrusting or abdominal crunching with these events.
